seo.title: AI Agent Hallucination Detection in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent hallucination detection in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Haystack pipelines, AI agent hallucination manifests when the Generator component produces responses that deviate from the retrieved context provided by the Retriever, often due to insufficient or irrelevant documents. This is exacerbated in RAG pipelines where type mismatches between components or silent errors in custom nodes allow fabricated information to propagate unchecked. Haystack's composable nature makes it prone to these issues, as pipelines can chain multiple LLM calls without built-in hallucination guards.

howItManifests.symptom: Generator outputs confident answers citing non-existent facts from empty/irrelevant context
howItManifests.impact: Users receive authoritative but completely fabricated responses

howItManifests.symptom: Response contains information never present in any retrieved documents
howItManifests.impact: Complete fabrication of facts, sources, and statistics

howItManifests.symptom: Generator receives malformed context (None, empty dict) and hallucinates freely
howItManifests.impact: Silent failures cascade through pipeline producing garbage outputs

howItManifests.symptom: Document preprocessing fails but pipeline continues with empty context
howItManifests.impact: Generator fabricates entire responses from scratch

detection.name: Context Faithfulness Check
detection.description: Detects when generated response contains information not present in retrieved context using Haystack's Document scoring
detection.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.generators import OpenAIGenerator
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever

# In your Haystack pipeline
def detect_hallucination(response: str, documents: list) -> bool:
    context_text = ' '.join([doc.content for doc in documents])
    # Simple n-gram overlap check
    response_ngrams = set(response.lower().split())
    context_ngrams = set(context_text.lower().split())
    overlap_ratio = len(response_ngrams.intersection(context_ngrams)) / len(response_ngrams)
    return overlap_ratio < 0.3  # Hallucination threshold

detection.name: Retriever Relevance Scoring
detection.description: Checks if retrieved documents actually contain query-relevant information before generation

detection.name: Self-Consistency Verification
detection.description: Generates multiple responses and checks for consistency against retrieved context

fixes.name: Context-Grounded Prompting
fixes.description: Forces generator to strictly adhere to retrieved context with explicit instructions
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.builders import PromptBuilder

context_prompt = PromptBuilder(
    template="""
    Answer ONLY using the following context. If the answer isn't in the context, say 'Insufficient information':
    
    Context: {{join(key='content', documents=documents, separator='\n')}}
    
    Query: {{query}}
    
    Answer:"""
)

generator = OpenAIGenerator(model='gpt-4o-mini')

rag_pipeline = Pipeline()
rag_pipeline.add_component('prompt_builder', context_prompt)
rag_pipeline.add_component('generator', generator)
rag_pipeline.connect('prompt_builder', 'generator')

fixes.name: Fallback Empty Context Handler
fixes.description: Prevents generation when retriever returns insufficient context

fixes.name: Document Pre-Validation
fixes.description: Filters out irrelevant or empty documents before generation

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Hallucination Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a custom Haystack component to automatically detect hallucination across all pipeline runs. It monitors retriever outputs, generator responses, and context faithfulness in real-time, flagging issues before they reach production. Vex provides detailed diagnostics including hallucination scores, context overlap metrics, and remediation suggestions specific to Haystack pipelines.

The Vex Haystack component wraps your existing RAG pipeline, injecting hallucination checks at critical junctions without requiring pipeline rewrites. It automatically generates test suites targeting your specific failure modes and provides one-click fixes through Haystack-compatible components. Vex's continuous monitoring catches regressions as you iterate on prompts, retrievers, or models.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexHallucinationMonitor
from haystack import Pipeline

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

# 1. Wrap existing pipeline with Vex monitor
rag_pipeline = Pipeline()
# ... your existing components ...

vex_monitor = VexHallucinationMonitor(
    failure_modes=['context_drift', 'fact_fabrication'],
    thresholds={'faithfulness': 0.8, 'relevance': 0.6}
)

# 2. Add to pipeline
rag_pipeline.add_component('vex_monitor', vex_monitor)
rag_pipeline.connect('retriever', 'vex_monitor.retriever_input')
rag_pipeline.connect('vex_monitor.generator_input', 'generator')

# 3. Run with automatic hallucination detection
result = rag_pipeline.run({
    'query': 'What is Haystack?',
    'vex_monitor': {'api_key': 'your-vex-api-key'}
})

# Vex auto-generates remediation code
print(result['vex_monitor']['remediation_code'])

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add Vex to your Haystack pipeline in 3 lines of code to eliminate hallucinations automatically.
